name: AXS Exhaustion Reacceptance Scaler v1
edge_mechanism: exhaustion reversal only after structure reaccepts prior value, with staged profit-taking rather than all-or-nothing exits
thesis: AXS 1h is structurally different and currently full CHOP. That makes it useful for a denser exhaustion-reacceptance test where the edge comes from overextension snapping back once value is reclaimed, while management scales out instead of demanding a single perfect reversal.
invalidation_condition: If AXS overshoots do not normalize after reacceptance, or if thinner structure makes reversals too noisy to survive costs, the thesis fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price extends into local exhaustion, reclaims prior value, and mean-reverts in stages rather than one clean continuous swing.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: In fully choppy 1h tapes, overshoots frequently reverse once the market re-enters prior balance. Structured scale-out lets the strategy monetize partial reversals instead of requiring large clean swings.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: AXS 1h is appropriate because it exaggerates short-term overshoots, making exhaustion and reacceptance more visible than on majors.

indicator_role_map.EMA_20: secondary structure reference for runner exit

pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: AXS may be too noisy, causing frequent reclaim prints that do not carry even to first target.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If first target hits but runner expectancy is poor, shrink the runner and emphasize earlier scale-out rather than tightening entry.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_trade_density: High relative to the other concepts in this batch.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Many small and medium reversals, with edge arising from repeated partial captures and limited runner dependence.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: AXS 1h trades frequently enough to matter and first two scale-out targets are reached often enough to clear costs.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, contradictory behavior versus chop thesis, or first target hit rate too low to support scale-out logic.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: low; stretch plus reacceptance should recur often on AXS 1h

validation_basket.why_included: Best lane in the basket for visibly exaggerated exhaustion and snap-back behavior.
validation_basket.continue_if: AXS produces frequent reacceptance reversals with positive first-target capture.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: AXS noise breaks the relationship between stretch and value reclaim.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: High participation with many partial-profit captures and modest runners.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the same exhaustion-reacceptance logic survives on a cleaner asset.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH keeps the same behavior with lower but still credible density.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: Only AXS exhibits the setup and ETH shows no real reacceptance response.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Lower count than AXS but cleaner scale-out behavior.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the logic survives slower structure without returning to zero-trade ceremony.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH 4h still produces enough reacceptance events to validate the mechanism.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h becomes too sparse to matter.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Reduced count but the same staged reversal profile.

long: Exit remaining 25% on touch of EMA_20 or 5 bars
short: Exit remaining 25% on touch of EMA_20 or 5 bars
